problem: find the time a ball fall onto the ground at height H.

plan.step: Identify the physics formula for free fall
plan.detailed_task: Recall that for an object in free fall from rest at height H, the time to hit ground is t = sqrt(2H/g) where g is gravitational acceleration (~9.8 m/s²)
Set up the height H as input variable and gravitational constant g = 9.8 m/s², ensuring units are consistent
Create a script or use shell to compute t = sqrt(2*H/g) for a given H value
Output the calculated fall time in seconds with appropriate precision
